questions.question: What is the primary function of a serial interface in digital design?
options: A) To transmit data in parallel format
B) To transmit data in serial format
C) To control external devices
D) To store data in memory
questions.explanation: Serial interfaces transmit data in a single wire, one bit at a time, making them suitable for long-distance transmission.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of using a D-flip-flop in digital design?
options: A) It can store two bits of data
B) It can store one bit of data
C) It can be used as a memory element
D) It can be used as a logic gate
questions.explanation: D-flip-flops can be used as memory elements, making them useful for storing data in digital systems.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a clock signal in digital design?
options: A) To transmit data
B) To control the flow of data
C) To generate a timing reference
D) To store data
questions.explanation: Clock signals provide a timing reference for digital systems, ensuring that data is transmitted and processed in a synchronized manner.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a combinational logic circuit?
options: A) It can store data
B) It can be used as a memory element
C) It produces a single output based on multiple inputs
D) It produces multiple outputs based on multiple inputs
questions.explanation: Combinational logic circuits produce an output based on one or more input signals, without storing any data.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a flip-flop in digital design?
options: A) To generate a clock signal
B) To transmit data
C) To store data
D) To control external devices
questions.explanation: Flip-flops are memory elements that store data for a specific amount of time, making them useful for digital systems.
